mentions.note: Oil plays are paradoxical to time on Trump rhetoric: when he says talks are going well, oil weakens (sell); when he warns of escalation, oil rallies (buy). Today's Iran strike cancellation drove oil lower. Focus on fundamentals and domestic supply insulation rather than geopolitical headlines.
Oil price volatile with Iran headlines. Trump's contradictory statements (seize Kharg Island vs. cancel strikes) create reverse-signal dynamics—buy on bad news, sell on good news rhetoric.
Preferred large-cap oil play over BP.
Iran conflict pushing oil prices; 3.8% dividend yield attractive at elevated commodity levels; strong cash flow generation; Mike Wirth running company; pull the trigger on weakness.
